rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use visible feature names and concrete bounds when defining range assumptions and guarantees in component type contracts.
generic_rules: Ensure that AGREE annex content respects the AADL scoping rules for component types and implementations.
Validate that all referenced identifiers in AGREE annex blocks are visible within the target scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use boolean expressions to define range conditions in AGREE: e.g., eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;
Place annex blocks in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level AGREE bindings in component type scopes or using subcomponent ports in type contracts.
Do not reference identifiers that are not visible in the current AADL scope.
